blueprint.causal_thesis: The BOJ Policy Board votes on a proposal to raise the uncollateralized overnight call rate from 0.75% to 1.0%. A simple majority of the 9 members is required. The outcome hinges on whether at least 5 members vote for the hike, given the 6-3 split at the prior meeting with three dissenting hawks. Mar

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members are recorded as absent; their vote is not counted; quorum must still be met.

latent_beliefs: Inflation must be sustainably at target with wage growth
Premature tightening could derail recovery
Ueda believes the BOJ must proceed cautiously, waiting for more data on wages, consumption, and inflation before raising rates again.
He is not convinced that the June meeting provides sufficient evidence, despite market expectations.
Market expectations for a hike are high, but internal hawks are pushing;
Ueda feels pressure to balance both.
latent_goals: Maintain credibility of gradual normalization
Avoid policy error
public_facts: (reported) [2024-03-19] March 19, 2024: BOJ raised rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1%. — “The Bank decided to change the guideline for market operations... the uncollateralized overnight call rate to be around 0 to 0.1%.”
(reported) [2024-07-31] July 31, 2024: BOJ raised rate to 0.25%. — “The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.25%.”
(reported) [2025-01-24] January 24, 2025: BOJ raised rate to 0.5%. — “The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.5%.”
(reported) [2025-03-19] March 19, 2025: BOJ raised rate to 0.75%. — “The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.75%.”
(confirmed) Polymarket implied probability for 25bp hike — “Polymarket implied probability for a 25 basis point hike stands at approximately 63%.”

(confirmed) [2024-03-19] The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, having raised rates from negative territory to 0.75% since March 2024, with a cautious pace dependent on data and inflation outlook. — “The Bank decided to change the guideline for market operations... the uncollateralized overnight call rate to be around 0 to 0.1%.”

(confirmed) [2026-04-28] At the April 2026 meeting, three board members dissented in favor of a hike to 1.0%, indicating internal pressure for tightening. — “Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.”

(confirmed) [2026-05-12] Market-implied probability of a June hike is around 63% as of mid-May 2026, with Reuters reporting the BOJ has signaled a possible hike. — “The Bank of Japan has locked in signals for a June rate hike, sources say.”

(reported) [2026-04-28] April 27-28, 2026 meeting: Nakagawa Junko proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated. — “Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.”

(reported) [2026-04-28] April 27-28, 2026 meeting: Takata Hajime proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated. — “Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.”

(reported) [2026-04-28] April 27-28, 2026 meeting: Tamura Naoki proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated. — “Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.”
